title: selliguea albopes
side_effects: Selliguea albopes can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems due to the plant's toxic compounds. Prolonged use or excessive consumption of the plant can lead to liver damage, kidney failure, and even coma in severe cases. Other potential side effects include dizziness, nausea, and headaches, which can be caused by the plant's psychoactive properties. In rare instances, improper use of Selliguea albopes can also trigger seizures, anxiety attacks, and long-term mental health issues.
